commit.message:
fix: replace '/' separators in tree item IDs with '.' to fix indentation (#430)

The slash character '/' in tree item IDs can interfere with VS Code's
internal tree handle construction, causing incorrect indentation:
- WestWorkspaceView: filesystem paths in IDs created accidental hierarchy
- ProjectConfigView: child IDs starting with parent ID + '/' confused
  VS Code's tree state tracking

commit.message:
fix: SVG logo rendering and player selection change/unselect (#2)

- Add explicit width/height attrs to all SVG files for proper img rendering
- Switch App.tsx motion import from framer-motion to motion/react (consistent)

  animation sticking at 0 on image elements

  other player changes selection

  and fix recursive clearing bug (track prevWinnerId before nulling)

commit.message:
redesign: Strip back to essentials — minimal but effective

Removed: metrics cards, tabs, timeline chart, sort dropdown, view toggle,
favourites, MC pills, platform icons, countdown, animated counters,
share button, sticky toolbar. All were adding clutter without core value.

Kept: Hero + status bar, search, status/product filters, product chips,
clean list rows with AI summaries, delayed badges, load-more pagination,
back to top.

Moved to footer: CSV download, RSS link, Browse by Product, freshness.

JS: 29KB -> 13KB (55% smaller)
CSS: 67KB -> 58KB (13% smaller)
Controls before content: 30 -> 3

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Simplify pipeline: single flow FRD→JSON→RDL/PBIP/.md

- generate_all.py: single 4-step pipeline (parse→rdl→pbip→spec);
  JSON is the human-editable checkpoint; .md specs are review docs only.
  Removed --approach flag and spec_reader round-trip entirely.
- src/spec_generator.py: new — FRD→.md review doc generator (inlined
  from frd-to-rdl-spec skill; no external dependency).
- src/config.py: new — reads pbi.properties; provides ConnectString,
  dataset GUIDs, logo base64, RDL layout defaults.
- pbi.properties: new — Fabric workspace, dataset GUIDs, RDL defaults,
  template paths.
- templates/: add MO_Report_Template.rdl (logo source) and spec .md
  templates.
- src/rdl_generator.py: production-quality RDL with header/footer/logo,
  correct ConnectString from pbi.properties.
- assets/pipeline.svg: rebuilt to show single flow with JSON checkpoint,
  three generator fork, pbi.properties config input.
- assets/banner.svg: add .md output node to decorative pipeline.
- README.md: full rewrite — pipeline table, JSON checkpoint workflow,
  configuration guide, updated project structure, troubleshooting.

commit.message:
v1.21.16: reject untrusted domains in SearXNG Strategy 2 EAN lookup

EAN numbers can appear in trademark, patent, and legal database pages.
SearXNG's Strategy 2 was blindly using those pages' titles as product names,
producing results like 'GODLY GOAL-GETTER Trademark Application' while the
Kesko CDN correctly returned the real product image.

Fix: Strategy 2 now only uses titles from a trusted-domain whitelist
(k-ruoka.fi, s-kaupat.fi, foodie.fi, barcodelookup.com, openfoodfacts.org,
etc.). Untrusted domains fall through to Strategy 3 which returns
'Unknown product (EAN)' with the correct image.

Also synced grocy_scraper_addon copy of searxng_client.py.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update src/static/app.js

commit.message:
fix: VRAM estimation from catalog + LLM re-plan fallback for failed edits

model_selector: Populate _VRAM_MAP and _MAX_CTX from model_catalog instead
of maintaining a separate hardcoded list. Add qwq:32b, qwen3:32b, and
devstral to COMPLEX_MODELS tier. Improve the fallback heuristic to parse
parameter counts from model names (e.g. 'qwq:32b' → ~20 GiB).

agent_loop: Add LLM-assisted re-plan as a third fallback in _act_edit_file.
When both exact and fuzzy matching fail (common with reasoning models like
qwq:32b that hallucinate file contents during planning), the agent now sends
the actual file content to the LLM and asks it to produce corrected old/new
text before giving up.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(jit): zero-cost context swap via Rc::into_raw/from_raw for closure calls

Eliminate atomic Rc inc/dec on every closure call by caching a raw
pointer with one refcount in CachedCalleeEntry. Context swaps use
Rc::from_raw → mem::replace → Rc::into_raw, avoiding 2 atomic ops
per call (~1000 calls in closure_counter benchmark).

Also cache skip_args flag to avoid parameter_count() virtual call
per iteration for 0-param closures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: set ENV PORT to match EXPOSE in web/user-dashboard/game-arena Dockerfiles

Railway routes traffic to the EXPOSE port. These services had EXPOSE set
to their default ports (3000/6001/8080) but Railway was injecting PORT=3001,
causing the app to listen on 3001 while Railway routed to the EXPOSE port.
Set ENV PORT to match EXPOSE so Railway injects the correct routing port.

Fixes 502 for web, user-dashboard, and game-arena on Railway.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add lightspeed commands to skip update check for fast exit (#7629)

* feat: add lightspeed commands to skip update check for fast exit

Extensions using AzureDeveloperCLICredential spawn 'azd auth token' as a
subprocess with a 10-second timeout. The background update check goroutine
(60s timeout) was blocking process exit in main.go, causing 'signal: killed'
errors when the update check was slow (e.g., after laptop wake, DNS stalls).

This change adds a Lightspeed property to ActionDescriptorOptions that marks
commands requiring fast exit. The update check goroutine is now started inside
ExecuteWithAutoInstall after command identification — lightspeed commands skip
it entirely, so the process exits immediately after producing output.

Changes:
- Add Lightspeed bool field to ActionDescriptorOptions
- Propagate lightspeed to cobra annotations in CobraBuilder
- Move fetchLatestVersion from main.go into startUpdateCheck() in auto_install.go
- Start update check only after rootCmd.Find(), skip for lightspeed commands
- Return ExecuteResult from ExecuteWithAutoInstall with LatestVersion channel
- Mark 'auth token' as Lightspeed: true

Fixes #7360

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: add lightspeed to cspell dictionary

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback

- Restore parse-error log for invalid AZD_SKIP_UPDATE_CHECK values
- Accept context parameter in startUpdateCheck for tracing/cancellation
- Set AZD_SKIP_UPDATE_CHECK in test to avoid real HTTP calls

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct LatestVersion doc comment to match behavior

The channel is nil only for lightspeed commands (goroutine never starts).
When AZD_SKIP_UPDATE_CHECK skips the check, the channel is closed without
a value rather than being nil.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: avoid real credential stack in lightspeed detection test

Use --output unknown to fail fast before touching the credential/provider
stack. The test only needs to verify IsLightspeed detection, not actual
token acquisition.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: assert LatestVersion nil/non-nil in lightspeed test

Directly test the core behavioral change: lightspeed commands must have
LatestVersion == nil (no goroutine started), non-lightspeed commands must
have LatestVersion != nil (goroutine started, even when AZD_SKIP_UPDATE_CHECK
short-circuits it).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: extract lightspeed annotation key to shared constant

Define AnnotationLightspeed = "azd.lightspeed" in cmd/actions package
and reference it from cobra_builder.go, auto_install.go, and tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Clocktower] Define turn state types and game phases (#450)

Defines the TypeScript types for Clocktower's turn state and phase
system as the foundational layer for the Clocktower game mode
implementation.

### New files

- `src/lib/game/modes/clocktower/types.ts` — all turn state and phase
types
- `src/lib/game/modes/clocktower/index.ts` — barrel export

### Types

- **`ClocktowerPhase` enum** — `Night | Day` discriminant
- **`ClocktowerNightPhase` / `ClocktowerDayPhase`** — discriminated
union members; `ClocktowerNightPhase` tracks `currentActionIndex` and
`nightActions` (keyed by role ID); `ClocktowerDayPhase` carries
`nominations`, `nominatedByPlayerIds`, and `executedToday`
- **`ClocktowerTurnPhase`** — `ClocktowerNightPhase |
ClocktowerDayPhase` union
- **`ClocktowerNightAction`** — primary target record for a role's night
action; intentionally minimal since Clocktower night resolution is
Storyteller-mediated rather than system-computed
- **`ClocktowerNomination` / `ClocktowerNominationVote`** — nomination
with per-player vote tally, supporting ghost votes and sequential public
voting
- **`ClocktowerTurnState`** — full turn state capturing `playerOrder`
(for neighbor abilities), `deadPlayerIds`, `ghostVotesUsed`,
`poisonedPlayerId`, `drunkPlayerId`, and `demonPlayerId`

### Notes

**Day-scoped fields on `ClocktowerDayPhase`**: `nominations`,
`nominatedByPlayerIds`, and `executedToday` live on the day phase rather
than top-level `ClocktowerTurnState` — they are day-scoped data that
naturally resets each phase transition.

**`poisonedPlayerId` and `drunkPlayerId`** are modeled separately:
poisoning is transient (one night + following day), while
`drunkPlayerId` is persistent and hidden from the affected player.

```ts
// Phase narrowing example
function handlePhase(state: ClocktowerTurnState) {
  if (state.phase.type === ClocktowerPhase.Night) {
    // state.phase.nightActions, state.phase.currentActionIndex available
  } else {
    // Day: state.phase.nominations, state.phase.executedToday available
  }
}
```

---------

fix: make auto-created backup detection resilient to status update failures

Use owner reference (via metav1.IsControlledBy) as fallback to detect
auto-created KopiaBackup objects when Status.AutoCreated may not have
been persisted (e.g., status update failed after initial Create).

Applies to both the deletion path (label removal) and the sync path
(schedule updates), ensuring the controller correctly manages backups
even after transient API errors.

commit.message:
fix: harden credit/billing idempotency edge cases

- billing_service.handle_checkout_completed: validate session.id is non-empty
  before building idempotency_key. A missing session.id would silently build a
  non-unique key (stripe_checkout__N) that could cause a false duplicate skip on
  a second checkout for the same credit amount. Now logs an error and returns
  early instead of risking incorrect crediting.

- credit_service.adjust_credits: add optional idempotency_key param to prevent
  admin double-submission from crediting a user twice. Admin UI can pass a
  frontend-generated UUID as idempotency_key on the POST body.

- admin.py CreditAdjustRequest: expose idempotency_key field so frontend can
  pass it through.

- credit_service.adjust_credits: add with_for_update() lock on user row
  (consistency with add_credits and deduct_credits).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

pass.

commit.message:
refactor: consolidate status updates into single deferred call

Replace scattered Status().Update() calls throughout reconcile methods
with a single deferred call in the main reconcile function. Sub-methods
now only set conditions in-memory; the defer persists them atomically.
Applies to both KopiaBackup and KopiaRepository controllers.

This eliminates data loss from mid-reconcile status update conflicts and
reduces API server calls per reconciliation cycle.

commit.message:
v0.3.20: transfer stock during pack conversion in optimizer

When optimizer merges a multi-pack product into its base product, it now
moves all stock entries to the base product with amount × pack_size before
deleting the multi-pack. Previously, DELETE FROM products cascaded into the
stock table, silently dropping all existing stock.

commit.message:
fix: atomic webhook claim + credit row-lock to eliminate race conditions

- billing_service: replace is_event_processed+mark_event (check-then-insert,
  not race-safe) with claim_webhook_event (INSERT, catch IntegrityError) +
  update_webhook_status (UPDATE only). Two concurrent Stripe retries for the
  same event_id can no longer double-process: DB UNIQUE constraint on
  stripe_event_id is the synchronization gate.

- billing router: use claim_webhook_event/update_webhook_status. Legacy
  is_event_processed/mark_event kept as deprecated aliases.

- credit_service.add_credits: SELECT user with with_for_update() to prevent
  concurrent credit additions from reading stale balance.

- credit_service.deduct_credits: re-fetch user with with_for_update() inside
  the deduction transaction instead of trusting the potentially-stale caller-
  provided user object. Prevents concurrent AI requests from both passing the
  balance check and double-deducting.

- tests/test_credits.py: update mocks to reflect the re-fetch pattern
  (side_effect list instead of single return_value).

All 12 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(direct): implement direct connection mode (no relay) (#19)

* feat(direct): implement direct connection mode (no relay)

Add direct connection mode allowing Yandex to call MA server directly
via HTTPS without the yaha-cloud.ru WebSocket relay.

New files:
- provider/direct.py: DirectConnectionHandler with HTTP routes for
  Smart Home API endpoints and OAuth account linking
- tests/test_direct.py: 36 tests covering auth, health, API, OAuth,
  and plugin integration

Modified:
- provider/plugin.py: Add _start_direct_mode() and cleanup in unload()
- provider/__init__.py: Add direct mode config entries with endpoint
  URLs, OAuth URLs, skill_id/skill_token fields
- provider/constants.py: Add direct-mode constants (paths, OAuth, etc.)
- README.md, CLAUDE.md: Document direct connection mode

The handler registers dynamic routes on MA's built-in webserver using
mass.webserver.register_dynamic_route(). OAuth implements minimal
Authorization Code flow for Yandex account linking.

commit.message:
fix: Fix CrateMess black screen caused by decompilation bug in OpenAndWait/CloseAndWait

The delegate in OpenAndWait and CloseAndWait was setting a throwaway
variable 'flag' instead of the loop condition (isDoneOpening/isDoneClosing),
causing an infinite loop. This meant CrateMess would hang forever with the
FadeView open after selecting a level, showing only a black screen.

commit.message:
chore(agent-logs): structured JSON log lines for agent logs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove unsupported upper() from provision workflow

GitHub Actions expressions support format() but not upper(), so the
workflow failed to parse before any job could start.

Use a supported conditional expression to select the PROD/TEST secret
and variable names, then reuse those values via job-level env vars.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: don't remove finalizer when user cleanup fails

Return error from handleFinalizer when DeleteUser fails, keeping the
finalizer in place so controller-runtime retries cleanup. Previously
the finalizer was removed regardless, leaking Kopia server users.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix mtg-forge-local port (8080) and update RAG setup docs

- Fix RagPipelineSettings.cs default BaseUrl: localhost:5000 → localhost:5001
- Fix appsettings.json RagPipeline BaseUrl: railway internal port 5000 → 8080
- Update RAILWAY-RAG-SETUP.md:
  - Section 3: point to mtg-forge-card-ai repo, correct env var names and port
  - Ingestion steps: use port 8080, add optional body params (limit/mongoOnly/qdrantOnly)
  - Section 4: fix RagPipeline__BaseUrl to use port 8080

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add all project files: specs, assets, build scripts, design system

- assets/: door photos, home.jpeg, ztl-sign.png, fonts (Cormorant + Jost)
- specs/001-014: all spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md, checklists
- build.sh: asset injection script
- .github/: copilot instructions + speckit agents
- .specify/: constitution v1.0.0 + speckit config

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: raise MAX_MAGLEV_DEOPT_RETRIES from 10 to 1000

10 deopts permanently disabled Maglev. During Criterion's 1-second
warmup, cold JIT ICs can easily trigger 10+ deopts, killing Maglev
for the entire measurement phase. With 1000 retries, exponential
backoff (capped at 1024 invocations) handles transient deopts while
still allowing Maglev to stabilize.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: move env/pkgs/registry setup into grazhda.sh, remove zgard init

1. Move .grazhda.env from $HOME to $GRAZHDA_DIR — all source code
   now resolves env file path within the Grazhda installation directory.

2. Copy .grazhda.pkgs.yaml into $GRAZHDA_DIR during both fresh install
   (grazhda.sh) and upgrade (grazhda upgrade).

3. Add version field to PackageDef in the registry schema — each package
   definition can now carry a default version.

4. Remove zgard init command — all infrastructure setup (mkdir pkgs,
   create .grazhda.env, copy registry, patch init script) now happens
   automatically during grazhda.sh installation and grazhda upgrade.

5. $GRAZHDA_DIR/pkgs directory is always created during installation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(security): prevent shell injection via unquoted CRD fields

Add shellQuote() helper using POSIX single-quote escaping to sanitize
all user-controlled values interpolated into shell scripts:
- Hostname, Username in filesystem and SFTP server scripts
- SFTPOptions.Host, Path in SFTP connect/create commands
- KnownHostsData via heredoc instead of echo with double quotes
- SSHCommand in external SSH flag
- Admin user setup (Username@Hostname)
- ExtraArgs in server start command

commit.message:
fix: Restrict Biome Includes to Supported File Types (#179)

Biome's `files.includes: ["**"]` caused biome to discover and parse
markdown files, triggering a panic in biome_markdown_parser on Chinese
text (byte boundary error). Restrict includes to only the file types
biome should actually process (JSON, JS/TS, CSS).

commit.message:
v0.3.19: preserve recipe-linked group-masters during optimizer parent cleanup

_strip_parents now checks recipe_ingredients before deleting group-master
products. Parents that are referenced by a recipe ingredient are kept in
the DB (but still deactivated + excluded from AI) so recipe links survive
the optimization run. Only orphan group-masters with no recipe references
are deleted.
